description:
Goal: finish the compiler2 source-authority swap so code/module/protocol indexing and source-consuming job state operate from rooted Fz AnyValueRef AST surfaces plus stable source identity, while treating the explicit legacy_fn_def seam from fz-rh2.11.7.14 as a temporary derived compatibility artifact rather than canonical source authority.

Grounding:
- fz-rh2.11.7.12 moved compiler2 source state onto quoted readers for code/module/function surfaces, but CodeState::Indexed and ModuleSource still retain legacy parser payloads for scope/module discovery.
- fz-rh2.11.7.14 made FunctionSource the authoritative function fact and made legacy_fn_def an explicit compiler2-owned one-way derived artifact instead of hidden parser authority.
- quoted_surface.rs and jobs/source.rs still pair quoted roots with legacy code/module/protocol items and attrs in order to discover alias/import/module/protocol/function forms.
- top-level and module attribute handling, protocol callback discovery, and module/protocol surface walking still need compiler2-owned quoted readers before old parser payloads can disappear from code/module state.
- downstream jobs/body.rs, jobs/contract.rs, jobs/dispatch.rs, and related keying helpers still consume legacy_fn_def today; that cleanup is tracked explicitly in fz-rh2.11.7.15 and is no longer part of this ticket.

Signal:
- IndexCode records rooted quoted source plus stable identity and publishes CodeIndexed without storing Rc<Item> as canonical code/module discovery state.
- ScopeCode and DefineModule discover attrs, aliases, imports, functions, modules, protocols, protocol impls, structs, and macro-call placeholders through compiler2 AST readers over quoted AnyValueRef roots.
- module/protocol source state stores only quoted-root authority plus the narrow non-source metadata needed to drive the graph.
- a function/module source-root identity change republishes the expected facts and wakes dependent jobs even if no old Rust AST pointer changed.
- rebuilding equal quoted source into different heap addresses does not by itself republish semantic facts.
- no compiler2 code/module indexing path calls the old parser to recover item/module/protocol discovery structure.

Change:
- replace code/module/protocol legacy discovery payloads with compiler2-owned quoted surface readers and narrow decoded surface records.
- teach quoted_surface.rs (or successor readers) to decode top-level/module attrs, module/protocol/protocol-impl surfaces, and protocol callbacks directly from quoted roots.
- remove old parser authority from IndexCode, ScopeCode, DefineModule, and related module discovery state.
- keep legacy_fn_def strictly as the explicit function-tier downstream compatibility seam from fz-rh2.11.7.14; do not regrow hidden parser bridges elsewhere.
- update telemetry/tests so they observe quoted-root source authority and revision behavior at the code/module/function tiers.

Why:
- macros and compiler services will produce quoted AnyValueRef roots; code/module discovery has to read the same rooted graph directly or the architecture silently regrows a second source authority.
- fz-rh2.11.7.14 already isolated the remaining downstream old-AST dependency behind an honest, named seam; this ticket should finish the authority swap around it, not mix that cleanup back in.

comments.text: Blocker discovered during implementation audit: swapping `IndexCode` / source authority to quoted roots right now would break compiler2 bootstrap. The current quoted front door in `src/compiler2/frontdoor.rs` does not yet parse core forms that already appear in compiler2-owned inputs, including `extern` item declarations and `if` expressions in `src/modules/runtime_library/kernel.fz`, plus `receive ... after`, expression lambdas, and richer pattern forms used by active fixtures/runtime modules. Using the old parser/AST as a fallback here would reintroduce the second-authority/conversion-loop problem this arc is removing, so I filed `fz-rh2.11.7.10` and blocked `.11.7.9` on it.

Implementation audit after fz-rh2.11.7.10/.11 showed that .9 is currently hiding two missing layers.

1. compiler2 still has no quoted-source reader/state layer for code/module/function surfaces: code.rs, identity.rs, and jobs/source.rs still store and walk Rc<Item>/FnDef directly.
2. contract/body/dispatch/keying still consume world.function_definition(...).ast, but compiler2 has no typed quoted-source body/pattern readers yet.

That means a direct .9 landing would either reintroduce old-parser authority in disguise or smuggle in an ad hoc converter at every downstream callsite. I split the seam into fz-rh2.11.7.12 (quoted source readers + source-state authority) and fz-rh2.11.7.13 (honest legacy_fn_def downstream artifact from quoted roots). Once those land, .9 can be the actual authority-swap closeout instead of a grab-bag of unrelated work.

Post-fz-rh2.11.7.14 correction: legacy_fn_def is now an explicit, derived, grep-able compatibility seam rather than hidden source authority. I filed fz-rh2.11.7.15 to own its later removal, and narrowed this ticket back to the actual remaining authority-swap work: code/module/protocol discovery and source state must stop depending on legacy parser payloads.

title: Compiler2 job model: consume source AST roots as AnyValueRef
